steps.title: Test the first pair
steps.content:
Substitute pair I, $(1,1)$, into $5x-3y<4$:
$$5(1)-3(1)=2$$
Since $2<4$, pair I satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test the second pair
steps.content:
For pair II, $(2,5)$, evaluate the left side:
$$5(2)-3(5)=-5$$
This value is less than $4$, so pair II works.

steps.title: Test the third pair
steps.content:
Substitute pair III, $(3,2)$:
$$5(3)-3(2)=9$$
Since $9<4$ is false, pair III does not satisfy the inequality.

steps.title: Select the matching statement
steps.content: The only ordered pairs that satisfy the inequality are I and II. That matches the choice listing I and II only, while excluding III. The answer is C.
